translation.en: The printer just ran out of ink.

notes_md:
**mực** = ink (the liquid used for writing or printing). It’s an uncountable noun, so you usually say *mực* without numbers; use **lọ/chai mực** for a bottle of ink.

Common pairings: **mực in** (printer ink), **mực viết** (writing ink), **hết mực** (run out of ink), **đổ mực** (spill ink).  
Very common second meaning: **mực** = squid (the seafood), totally different meaning but same spelling — context tells you which one.  
Memory tip: think “ink is dark like squid ink” to link both meanings.

translation.en: Don't spill ink on the table.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I bought more ink to replace in the office printer.
You accidentally spilled ink on my study desk last night.
I need ink to write a handwritten letter to my close friend.

definitions.vi: Một loại chất lỏng dùng để viết hoặc in.
definitions.en: A liquid used for writing or printing.
definitions.es: Un líquido utilizado para escribir o imprimir.

example.vi: Bút của tôi đã hết mực.
example.en: My pen has run out of ink.
example.es: Mi bolígrafo se ha quedado sin tinta.

notes:
mực = ink (the liquid used for writing or printing). It’s an uncountable noun, so you usually say mực without numbers; use lọ/chai mực for a bottle of ink.

Common pairings: mực in (printer ink), mực viết (writing ink), hết mực (run out of ink), đổ mực (spill ink).  
Very common second meaning: mực = squid (the seafood), totally different meaning but same spelling — context tells you which one.  
Memory tip: think “ink is dark like squid ink” to link both meanings.
exampleSentences: Anh mua thêm mực để thay cho máy in ở văn phòng.
Chị lỡ làm đổ mực lên bàn học của em tối qua.
Em cần mực để viết thư tay cho bạn thân.
